_**User**_

Look at the docs and readme for context on this branch. I have some github actions set up to run tests on pull requests. Right now, it runs three different actions every time - frontend, backend, and font catalogs. Is there a way to make this all happen under one main "test" action? Like how frontend tests runs unit-and-integration and e2e, can we have a main testing action that runs everything?

---

_**Agent (composer-2-fast)**_


Exploring the docs and GitHub Actions setup to see how workflows are structured and how we can consolidate them.

**Consolidating workflows**

I'm setting up branch-specific CI triggers for main and develop branches. I'm adjusting the CI setup to ensure font catalogs always run on PRs while preserving path filtering for frontend/backend workflows. I'm consolidating the CI workflows into a single file to simplify path filtering logic. 

I'm simplifying CI triggers by using a single `on` trigger that fires on every PR and letting the first job handle path filtering with `paths-filter`. I'm adjusting the CI workflow to run font catalog on every push/PR without path filtering to avoid skipping it during doc changes. I'm implementing path filtering for frontend/backend jobs to optimize CI runs while keeping one main test action. 

I'm setting up path-based filtering for CI jobs using the `actions/filter` action with frontend/backend conditions. I'm adjusting path-based filters to handle workflow changes and document-only PRs correctly.

I'm consolidating CI workflows using `dorny/paths-filter` to avoid duplicate runs while preserving the original behavior. I'm implementing `dorny/paths-filter` with conditional job dependencies, ensuring `font-catalogs` runs in parallel with `changes` without requiring path filtering. I'm updating the path filters to properly handle boolean string outputs from paths-filter and ensuring font-catalogs job skips when only docs change. I'm implementing the paths-filter syntax with frontend and backend patterns, using v3 action with fetch-depth: 0 for PRs to ensure proper checkout of changes.

I'm renaming the workflow to `ci.yml` to match the user's 'main test action' designation. I'm updating the workflow name to `tests.yml` and verifying the npm registry for dorny/paths-filter v3. I'm switching to dorny/paths-filter@v3 and realizing the workflow-level `paths:` filtering means no frontend/backend workflows run if only docs are changed. I'm consolidating workflows to avoid unnecessary triggering while ensuring docs changes still run font catalog jobs. 

I'm adjusting workflow triggers to run font catalog jobs on all PRs but skip frontend/backend jobs for docs-only changes. I'm implementing workflow_dispatch trigger and concurrency group to cancel duplicate runs. I'm updating workflow names to match the new directory structure. 

I'm verifying that `paths-filter` correctly exports `frontend`/`backend` values and checking the scripts path for `check-font-catalogs.mj`. I'm verifying the workflow file paths and confirming that font catalog checks run on every PR regardless of other changes. I'm updating the README and docs to reflect the single workflow name change and removing the old workflow files. I'm adding job names with `name:` for clarity in PR checks. </details></think>

_**Agent (composer-2-fast)**_


Here’s what was going on and what we did.

## Yes — one workflow, several jobs

GitHub Actions already supports this: **one workflow file** can define **multiple jobs** (like your old “Frontend tests” workflow with `unit-and-integration` and `e2e`). You don’t need three separate workflow runs for three separate YAML files.

## What changed

1. **New [`.github/workflows/tests.yml`](.github/workflows/tests.yml)** — workflow name **“Tests”** with jobs:
   - **`changes`** — runs [`dorny/paths-filter`](https://github.com/dorny/paths-filter) so frontend/backend only run when the same kinds of paths as before would have triggered those workflows.
   - **`font-catalogs`** — always (same as your old always-on font check).
   - **`frontend-unit-and-integration`** / **`frontend-e2e`** — only when the frontend filter matches.
   - **`backend`** — only when the backend filter matches.

   **`concurrency`** is set so new pushes on the same branch cancel an in-progress run of this workflow.

2. **Old three workflows** — file deletes were rejected, so those files are still there but are **deprecated**: they only run on **`workflow_dispatch`** (manual), with a short comment pointing at `tests.yml`. That avoids **double** CI (three old workflows + new one). If you’re fine removing them, delete the three old YAML files and rely only on `tests.yml`.

3. **[`README.md`](README.md)** and **[`docs/testing.md`](docs/testing.md)** — updated to describe the single **Tests** workflow.

## What you’ll see on PRs

- One workflow run titled **Tests** with sub-jobs (Font catalogs, Frontend …, Backend …). Skipped jobs show as skipped when paths don’t match.
- If **branch protection** required exact check names like “Frontend tests” or “Backend tests”, update those rules to the new names, e.g. **Tests / Frontend unit & integration**, **Tests / Backend tests**, etc.

If you want **every** PR to always run frontend and backend (no path skipping), we can drop the `changes` job and the `if:` conditions so all test jobs always run under **Tests**.

---

_**User**_

Is there a way to keep those files as they are but just have one main action file? There's an outside constraint that the run-frontend-tests and run-backend-tests files need to exist

---

**Setting up reusable workflows**

I'm implementing workflow_call to create reusable test workflows orchestrated by a main workflow. I'm implementing reusable workflows using `workflow_call` with a main entry point that calls test workflows. 

**Configuring workflow triggers**

I'm ensuring child workflows only trigger via `workflow_call` to prevent duplicate executions with main workflows. I'm implementing reusable workflows with main caller pattern to avoid duplicate executions. I'm switching to reusable workflows with `workflow_call:` for child workflows while keeping main workflow as the sole trigger source. I'm adjusting the workflow structure to preserve existing files while using a main action file with `workflow_call` triggers. I'm implementing a single main workflow with `workflow_call` to avoid multiple separate workflow runs in the UI. I'm clarifying how reusable workflows appear in the UI and understanding that caller workflows run the reusable workflows as jobs. 

Reusable workflows must use `workflow_call` trigger to be called by other workflows. I'm adjusting the workflow triggers to prevent duplicate runs by making the parent workflow the sole trigger for push/PR events. </details></think>

_**Agent (composer-2-fast)**_


Restoring `run-frontend-tests.yml` and `run-backend-tests.yml` as reusable workflows (`workflow_call`) and adding a parent workflow that calls them so we get one Actions run while keeping those files. Checking the repo's workflow files.
